_meta.purpose: Static-page content from the live WP site, captured for redesign. NOT a structural template — only the content text matters.
_meta.notes: Only 3 of 10 candidate slugs exist. /about-us/ and /contact/ both ship with Lorem ipsum placeholder copy in the editorial body — the live site never had real About / Contact prose written. Only /contributors/ has actual content. Treat the new Astro About/Contact pages as a green-field write, not a port.

pages.title: About Us
pages.content:
PLACEHOLDER — the live page body is Lorem ipsum, not real editorial copy. Verbatim excerpts of what is currently on the page:

Opening section: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Aliquam non mauris egestas diam ultrices hendrerit."

Featured quote block: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Aliquam non mauris egestas diam ultrices hendrerit. Curabitur vel massa sem."

"Wikilogy" section: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Aliquam non mauris egestas diam ultrices hendrerit."

The only real identifying statement that appears anywhere on the page is the site-wide tagline: "Maintained by the Centre for Civil Society (CCS) and supported by the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom."

IMPLICATION FOR REDESIGN: there is no legacy About prose to preserve. The new Astro About page should be written from scratch (likely by Arjun / Kumar Anand at CCS) and should at minimum cover: what Indian Liberals is, who maintains it (CCS), who funds it (FNF), what kinds of material it archives (periodicals, regional-language literature, opinion, audio/video), and the editorial mission.
links_found: Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom (attribution, no URL on page)
Centre for Civil Society / CCS (parent organization)

pages.title: Contact
pages.content:
Editorial body is essentially empty — a single "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet." placeholder line followed by a contact form. The substantive content on the page is the contact block itself:

Address: A-69, Hauz Khas, New Delhi -110 016
Email: editor@indianliberals.in
Phone: +91-11-26521882

Footer attribution on the page: "Supported by the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom."

IMPLICATION FOR REDESIGN: the contact details (address / email / phone) are the only load-bearing content and should carry over verbatim. Everything else on the contact page can be rebuilt in Astro with a proper short intro and either a mailto or a simple form.

pages.title: Contributors
pages.content:
Intro paragraph (verbatim, including the original typo): "Various individuals and organigations have helped us seeing this project to fruition. Some of them are mentioned below (name in alphabetical order):"

Contributor list (verbatim, alphabetical as on the live page):

- Asantha Sirimanne, Capital Media, Colombo
- Chandrasekaran Balakrishnan, Chennai
- J R Jayewardene Centre, Colombo
- Nupur Hasija, Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom, New Delhi
- S Divakara, Forum of Free Enterprise, Mumbai
- Swati Kapadia, Forum of Free Enterprise, Mumbai
- Bruce Caldwell, Duke University
- D R Pendse, Centre for Economic Policy Advice, Mumbai
- Minoo Shroff, A D Shroff Memorial Trust, Mumbai
- S V Raju, Freedom First, Mumbai
- Saurabh Modi, Hansraj College, New Delhi
- Weerakoon Wijewardena, ex-Deputy Governor, Central Bank of Sri Lanka
- Carol A Leadenham, Hoover Institution Archives, Stanford University
- G P Manish, Troy University
- Nanaji G Shewale, Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune
- Rajas Parchure, Gokhale Institute of Politics and Economics, Pune
- Sunil Bhandare, Tata Strategic Management Group, Mumbai
- Mohammed Anas Khan, Centre for Civil Society, New Delhi

IMPLICATION FOR REDESIGN: this is the only static page on the legacy site with genuinely portable content. The list is short enough to render as a clean credits page in Astro (alphabetical, with affiliation as a secondary line). The typo "organigations" should be silently fixed ("organisations"), and the awkward "seeing this project to fruition" can be tightened to "bringing this project to fruition" — flag to Arjun / Kumar Anand before changing.
